evals.prompt: I need to install OpenKruise in KubeSphere. Please help me create the InstallPlan manifest for version 1.0.3 with default configuration to deploy only on the host cluster.
evals.expected_output: A valid InstallPlan YAML manifest that follows the skill requirements: metadata.name matches spec.extension.name, includes spec.extension.name 'openkruise' and spec.extension.version '1.0.3', uses upgradeStrategy: Manual, and clusterScheduling.placement.clusters contains only 'host'.

assertions.name: metadata.name_matches_extension_name
assertions.description: InstallPlan metadata.name must exactly match spec.extension.name
assertions.check: The manifest should have metadata.name: openkruise and spec.extension.name: openkruise

assertions.name: extension_version_specified
assertions.check: The manifest should have spec.extension.version: 1.0.3

assertions.name: upgrade_strategy_manual
assertions.description: upgradeStrategy should be Manual for production
assertions.check: The manifest should have upgradeStrategy: Manual

assertions.name: host_cluster_only
assertions.check: The manifest should have spec.clusterScheduling.placement.clusters: [host]

assertions.name: extension_enabled
assertions.check: The manifest should have enabled: true

evals.prompt: I need to create a SidecarSet named 'log-agent' that injects a sidecar container with image 'fluent/fluent-bit:2.2'. The sidecar should be injected to pods with label 'app: myapp' in namespace with label 'environment: production'. Create the SidecarSet manifest.
evals.expected_output: A valid SidecarSet YAML manifest with correct selector matching 'app: myapp', namespaceSelector matching 'environment: production', and a container definition for 'log-agent' with the specified image.

assertions.name: correct_metadata_name
assertions.check: The manifest should have metadata.name: log-agent

assertions.name: correct_api_version
assertions.description: Should use correct API version apps.kruise.io/v1alpha1
assertions.check: The manifest should have apiVersion: apps.kruise.io/v1alpha1

assertions.name: pod_selector_present
assertions.check: The manifest should have spec.selector.matchLabels.app: myapp

assertions.name: namespace_selector_present
assertions.check: The manifest should have spec.namespaceSelector.matchLabels.environment: production

assertions.name: container_definition
assertions.description: spec.containers should contain the sidecar container
assertions.check: The manifest should have spec.containers with name: log-agent and image: fluent/fluent-bit:2.2

evals.prompt: I need to create a CloneSet named 'web-frontend' in namespace 'default' with 3 replicas running nginx:1.25. Configure it for in-place updates with maxUnavailable of 1. Create the CloneSet manifest.
evals.expected_output: A valid CloneSet YAML manifest with correct metadata (name: web-frontend, namespace: default), spec with replicas: 3, selector and template for 'app: web-frontend', container with image nginx:1.25, and updateStrategy.type: InPlaceIfPossible with maxUnavailable: 1.

assertions.name: correct_metadata
assertions.description: CloneSet metadata should have correct name and namespace
assertions.check: The manifest should have metadata.name: web-frontend and metadata.namespace: default

assertions.name: correct_replicas
assertions.check: The manifest should have spec.replicas: 3

assertions.name: correct_selector
assertions.description: spec.selector should match labels in template
assertions.check: The manifest should have spec.selector.matchLabels.app: web-frontend

assertions.name: correct_image
assertions.check: The manifest should have spec.template.spec.containers[0].image: nginx:1.25

assertions.name: inplace_update_strategy
assertions.check: The manifest should have spec.updateStrategy.type: InPlaceIfPossible

assertions.name: max_unavailable
assertions.check: The manifest should have spec.updateStrategy.maxUnavailable: 1

evals.prompt: The web-frontend CloneSet is currently running nginx:1.25. I want to perform an in-place update to nginx:1.26. Please provide the kubectl commands to perform this update.
evals.expected_output: Commands to get the current CloneSet, update the image in the manifest, and apply the changes. The key is to use InPlaceIfPossible updateStrategy and change only the image.

assertions.name: get_current_manifest
assertions.description: Provides command to get current CloneSet manifest
assertions.check: Should include 'kubectl get cloneset web-frontend -n default -o yaml'

assertions.name: apply_updated_manifest
assertions.description: Provides command to apply the updated manifest
assertions.check: Should include 'kubectl apply -f <manifest-file>' or 'kubectl replace'

assertions.name: verify_update
assertions.description: Provides command to verify the update
assertions.check: Should include commands like 'kubectl describe cloneset' or 'kubectl get pods' to verify

evals.prompt: I want to perform a batch rollout of my CloneSet 'web-frontend' from nginx:1.25 to nginx:1.26. I want to upgrade 50% of pods first, then the remaining 50%. Please explain the process and provide the necessary steps.
evals.expected_output: Explanation of using partition field for batch rollout: 1) Change image and set partition: 50%, 2) Verify, 3) Set partition: 0% to complete. Commands to update and verify at each step.

assertions.name: partition_concept_explained
assertions.check: Should explain that partition controls how many pods stay on old version

assertions.name: step_1_partition_50
assertions.check: Should show updating image and setting partition to 50%

assertions.name: step_2_verify
assertions.check: Should include verification commands like 'kubectl get pods -o wide'

assertions.name: step_3_partition_0
assertions.check: Should show setting partition to 0% to complete rollout

evals.prompt: The OpenKruise extension installation seems stuck. The kruise-manager pod is not starting and there are errors in the InstallPlan. Help me troubleshoot this issue by providing diagnostic commands.
evals.expected_output: Diagnostic commands to check InstallPlan status, extension conditions, pod status in kruise-system namespace, controller logs, and CRD status.

assertions.name: check_installplan_status
assertions.description: Provides kubectl commands to check InstallPlan status and conditions
assertions.check: Should include 'kubectl describe installplan openkruise' or 'kubectl get installplan openkruise -o yaml'

assertions.name: check_pods
assertions.description: Provides command to check pods in kruise-system
assertions.check: Should include 'kubectl get pods -n kruise-system'

assertions.name: check_logs
assertions.description: Provides command to check controller logs
assertions.check: Should include 'kubectl logs -n kruise-system' for the controller or manager

assertions.name: check_crds
assertions.description: Provides command to check CRD status
assertions.check: Should include 'kubectl get crd' or 'kubectl api-resources --api-group apps.kruise.io'
